summary: Add the T1b2 main-owned source-operation acceptance and dispatch-outbox metadata foundation.
rationale: Issue #346 adds schema v8 and four direct RuntimeControlStore transaction/read methods because the existing store is the stable owner of runtime-control SQLite migrations and atomic business writes. The implementation remains metadata-only and keeps typed records, validation, row conversion, and replay comparison in a focused source_operations module. RuntimeControlStore is already oversized; this bounded slice adds the required schema and transaction responsibility without introducing indirection solely to satisfy a line threshold. Store decomposition remains separate governance work.
line_count_rationale: RuntimeControlStore already owns runtime-control schema initialization, ordered migrations, and BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction boundaries. T1b2 extends that stable storage facade with one bounded schema and transaction surface; moving transaction ownership or compressing unrelated code would obscure the crash-atomic acceptance contract. A later dedicated cleanup may split the existing oversized store without widening issue #346.
deletion_targets: source dispatch acceptance without durable main-owned operation identity and initial authorization metadata
schema upgrades that can persist partial v8 source-operation DDL while user_version remains 7
ambiguous source-operation replay behavior across identity, idempotency, and acknowledgement races
verification: uv run --group dev python -m pytest -p no:tach tests/test_runtime_control_source_operations.py tests/test_runtime_control_store.py tests/test_runtime_control_event_contract.py -q
uv run --group dev ruff check src tests experiments tools
uv run --group dev ty check src tests tools
uv run --group dev python tools/check_pr_governance.py --base origin/main
risks: Existing v1 through v7 SQLite files must preserve prior rows and leave no partial v8 source-operation objects after an injected v8 DDL failure.
Exact replay must compare immutable acceptance and initial authorization metadata without resetting later ledger or acknowledgement heads.
This metadata foundation does not implement wire payload reconstruction, Source Port authentication, fence validation, redispatch, reconciliation, or production recovery.
